episode: 1973-1974 oil shock and equity bear market
core_pattern: A geopolitical oil disruption raised inflation while growth weakened, creating pressure on both consumers and company margins.
main_warning: A short-lived oil disruption is not equivalent to a prolonged embargo.

core_pattern: Central-bank credibility became the dominant market variable as policy stayed restrictive until inflation clearly weakened.
main_warning: Today's financial system, debt levels, and central-bank framework are different.

core_pattern: Oil rose sharply on supply fears, then reversed as the expected disruption proved less persistent than feared.
main_warning: Market outcomes depend more on physical supply than on alarming headlines.

core_pattern: Currency weakness and leveraged positions amplified one another, eventually forcing deleveraging across markets.
main_warning: Currency depreciation alone does not imply a systemic crisis.

core_pattern: A real technological shift attracted too much capital to companies whose future cash flows could not justify their prices.
main_warning: A bubble in some companies does not mean the underlying technology is unimportant.

core_pattern: Persistent end demand supported suppliers until new capacity eventually reduced pricing power.
main_warning: Supplier profits can peak before headline demand does.

core_pattern: Falling asset prices exposed leverage and weak balance sheets, turning a market decline into a financing crisis.
main_warning: An equity correction without credit stress is not automatically another 2008.

core_pattern: A change in expected monetary policy rapidly lifted yields and pressured assets dependent on cheap global liquidity.
main_warning: The speed of repricing can matter more than the absolute level of rates.

core_pattern: Weak demand expectations met excess supply, hurting commodity producers while benefiting many consumers.
main_warning: Lower commodity prices can be either a growth warning or a consumer tailwind.

episode: 2018 Fed tightening and fourth-quarter technology selloff
core_pattern: Higher discount rates and policy uncertainty compressed valuations even while many underlying businesses remained healthy.
main_warning: Valuation compression is different from permanent business impairment.

core_pattern: A severe economic shock caused indiscriminate selling before extraordinary policy support changed the market direction.
main_warning: This episode depended on an unusually fast and large policy response.

core_pattern: Inflation proved less temporary than expected, forcing rates higher and punishing long-duration growth assets.
main_warning: Not every increase in inflation becomes a multi-year inflation regime.

episode: 2023 regional-bank stress and AI-led market concentration
core_pattern: Rate pressure hurt weak balance sheets while investors concentrated in a small group of companies expected to benefit from AI.
main_warning: Concentration can persist longer than valuation arguments suggest.
